knowledge_need: Study topic as Engineer-Philosopher. Attempt 1 of up to 3. Give compact Builder-relevant understanding, validation needs, and return plan.

part_digests.name: core meaning
part_digests.meaning: An atom is a compact reusable learning claim; a case pattern is a contextual repeated situation that may contain useful evidence but is not yet generalized.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: X needs the Builder to separate durable learning from examples, so memory does not become either too abstract or too anecdotal.
compact_missing_knowledge: Exact local acceptance criteria for atom promotion
How current Builder stores and retrieves case patterns
compact_safe_learning_steps: Compare existing accepted atoms against source cases
Define a minimal invariant claim before promotion
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Show the same atom applies correctly to at least one non-source case
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X1 as the vocabulary and sorting rule for the rest of X.

part_digests.name: why I am not ideal
part_digests.meaning: The weakness is confusing repetition, salience, or recent success with validated reusable knowledge.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: This identifies the failure mode X is meant to reduce: premature abstraction or failure to abstract when learning from Builder experience.
compact_missing_knowledge: Known false promotions in prior Builder work
Known missed abstractions from repeated cases
compact_safe_learning_steps: Audit a small sample of memories for overgeneralization
Keep draft status until proof links exist
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Find whether proposed learning changes decisions without relying on the original case text
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X2 to define guardrails against bad atom creation.

part_digests.name: builder relevance
part_digests.meaning: Builder learning needs both layers: case patterns preserve situational texture; atoms provide compact decision material for future self-build or child-agent build.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: This makes X operational: the Builder should learn by retaining cases as evidence and promoting only stable, validated reusable atoms.
compact_missing_knowledge: Where child-agent build decisions currently consult retained learning
Whether retrieval favors atoms, cases, or both
compact_safe_learning_steps: Map one learning item from case evidence to candidate atom to decision reuse
Keep child-agent behavior changes behind validation gates
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Demonstrate retrieval of the learning item during a later relevant decision
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X3 to connect the distinction to actual Builder behavior, not just memory taxonomy.

part_digests.name: validation
part_digests.meaning: Understanding is valid only if the Builder can classify, retrieve, apply, and test the learning without corrupting route or runtime behavior.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: X needs proof that the distinction improves learning quality and future decisions, not just terminology.
compact_missing_knowledge: Available validator for atom retention and behavior delta
Baseline behavior before the learning item is introduced
compact_safe_learning_steps: Prefer small fixture cases before runtime use
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X4 as the acceptance gate before any promotion claim.

part_digests.name: promotion decision
part_digests.meaning: Default decision should be conservative: case pattern unless a minimal reusable claim has validation evidence; atom candidate if promising but not proven; open gap if criteria or evidence are missing.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: This is the practical output X likely needs: a promotion rule for Builder learning artifacts.
compact_missing_knowledge: Required evidence format for accepted memory
compact_safe_learning_steps: Label outputs as draft until validated
Record why a case pattern was not promoted
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Confirm promoted atom survives retention, retrieval, and correct reuse
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X5 to return X as a decision framework: case pattern, atom candidate, or open gap.

dependencies: X2 depends on X1 because the failure mode requires a clear atom/case distinction
X3 depends on X1 and X2 to make the distinction operational
X5 depends on X4 because promotion requires validation evidence
duplicates_or_overlaps: X4 overlaps X5: validation evidence is the basis for promotion
X1 overlaps X3: both define the distinction, but X3 applies it to Builder behavior

parent_return_plan.how_to_rebuild_x: Return X as a compact Builder learning rule: preserve case patterns as evidence, extract atom candidates only when a minimal invariant exists, and promote only after retrieval plus behavior-delta validation.
parent_return_plan.next_small_action: Take one known Builder learning example and classify it as case pattern, atom candidate, or open gap with explicit evidence links.
proof_needed: A decision reuse or behavior delta check

promotion_decision.owner_decision_required: only if promotion beyond case pattern is requested
promotion_decision.reason: Batch source answer is material only; default is compact case pattern with part map and parent return plan.
